cards.back: An iterative and incremental approach to project management and software development that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and rapid delivery.
A software development methodology that emphasizes technical practices such as pair programming, continuous integration, and refactoring to deliver high-quality software quickly and reliably.

Models such as Scrum, Kanban, Lean Software Development, Crystal, and Dynamic Systems Development Method (DSDM) that offer variations of the Agile framework to suit different project needs and team sizes.
A role responsible for facilitating the Scrum process, removing impediments, and ensuring the team follows the Agile principles and values.
Tools such as Jira, Trello, Asana, Microsoft Teams, and Slack that support Agile project management, team collaboration, and continuous delivery.
